source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
The following bar graph shows the relative biomass of four different populations of a particular food pyramid.
Which of the following would be the most likely result if there was an increase in the number of organisms in population C?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text: The biomass of population B will decrease.

choice_map.A: The biomass of population D will remain the same.
choice_map.B: The biomass of population B will decrease.
choice_map.C: The biomass of population C will steadily increase.
choice_map.D: The food source available to population C would increase.

extraction_notes: answer field is numeric "1" but the record does not make the choice indexing scheme explicit
image field contains a PIL image placeholder rather than a reusable path or URL
source_intake_record.subject: Biology

issues: Choice texts are missing: choice_count is 4 and metadata.choice_field is "choices", but no choices field is provided.
metadata.extraction_notes state the source answer was numeric "1" without an explicit choice indexing scheme.

image_dependency: Question explicitly refers to 'the following bar graph' to determine the food pyramid populations.
The needed relationship among populations A-D is not fully specified in text alone; it depends on reading the graph.
multi_step: Solving requires identifying population ordering/relative biomass from the bar graph, then inferring trophic effects of increasing population C.
The task involves at least two reasoning steps: graph interpretation and ecological cause-effect prediction.
The answer choices are conceptual alternatives rather than direct graph readout, so some inference beyond extraction is required.
verifiability: A single target answer is provided: 'The biomass of population B will decrease.'
Choices are present in the input, so the answer can be checked against option B.
asset_registry_record.registry_passed=true supports baseline data completeness, but listed registry issues introduce some ambiguity about original choice mapping.
